front: Painting: a nude woman descends a staircase in a blurred 'photo-painting' that is an homage to Duchamp
Painting: blurred photorealistic portraits of members of the Red Army Faction, officially titled October 18, 1977
Painting: source images included in a 4000-photo Atlas project
Painting: pixelated stained-glass window of 11,500 panes arranged randomly by a computer, designed for the largest Gothic church in northern Europe
back: Symphony of Light / Cologne Cathedral window (Gerhard Richter)

front: Painting: six square abstract paintings created using a squeegee, housed at the Tate Modern, named for an avant-garde composer
work: Abstract Works (Squeegee Paintings, Color Charts, Birkenau)

front: Painting: series of grid paintings with colors selected at random, part of 'color chart'-like compositions
work: Abstract Works (Squeegee Paintings, Color Charts, Birkenau)

front: Painting: four abstract works featured in the last-ever exhibition at the Met Breuer, a COVID-shortened retrospective called Painting After All
work: Abstract Works (Squeegee Paintings, Color Charts, Birkenau)

front: Artist: in 1994, used a carpet knife to divide a painting into 64 squares that were each sold separately under the title Souvenir
work: Abstract Works (Squeegee Paintings, Color Charts, Birkenau)

front: Artist: fictionalized in Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck's film Never Look Away
Artist: was once a member of Fluxus, a movement that also included Dick Higgins and Joseph Beuys
Painting: a candle painting that symbolizes death, appearing on the cover of Sonic Youth's album Daydream Nation
Painting: a 1988 portrait showing the artist's daughter looking away from the viewer
Artist: associated with the capitalist realism movement, which developed in 1960s Germany in response to pop art; fellow member Sigmar Polke
Artist: declared that gray is 'the epitome of non-statement' when describing his Grey Paintings
work: Abstract Works (Squeegee Paintings, Color Charts, Birkenau)

front: Artist: curated the canon of male Western minds for a room full of their images in his 48 Portraits
Artist: attended and taught at the Kunstakademie Dusseldorf, alongside photographers Bernd and Hilla Becher
Artist: before embracing abstractionism, worked in photorealism — a movement that also includes Richard Estes and Chuck Close
Artist: painting inspired by Reagan's Star Wars initiative, consisting of blotches of yellow, black over gray
work: Abstract Works (Squeegee Paintings, Color Charts, Birkenau)
